item.title: Comment
item.content:encoded:
Hi, I think this is very disturbing, but there's a little detail that bugs me. The SCP mentions a "Canaanite language" that the idol speaks in, that "probably" matches the idol's inscription.

If the team assigned to it can understand what the idol is saying when it speaks, wouldn't that mean they have positively identified the language some time ago?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So it caused 2020 huh?

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: For the record, I gratefully approve this change.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
The image that was used in this article has been replaced with an image that's cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) and is sourced as follows:

Title: scp-089-new.jpg
Author(s): Stella, Elogee FishTruck
Release year: 2020
Note: Created by Elogee FishTruck as an edited version of the image below.

Source: Wikimedia Commons
License: CC BY-SA 4.0
Title: Statua del dio Moloch, museo del cinema di Torino.jpg
Author: Stella

item.title: Different version?
item.content:encoded: Was there an earlier version of this? It had more gruesome details, as I recall. And at one point, the statue called for conjoined twins.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I’m going back and forth between upvoting and novoting here. Though I do like the way the piece builds up, the gut punch of the infant thing leaves a bad taste in my mouth, as it feels like it’s taking advantage of that emotion. But it’s so well built up…

I’ll leave it on an upvote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oof. Excellent job with the narrative build up. I knew you were keeping it vague for a reason and was prepared for something awful but it still turned my stomach when I hit the reveal. The guilt B would feel either way would be crippling.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: didn't mean to reply to this, sorry!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What would happen if 089-A or 089-B died before execution of protocol M8? And what would happen if protocol M8 is delayed so much that 089-A exceeded the size of the cavity by growing? And what if something went wrong in the M8 procedure that caused 089-A being destroyed and not possible to redo the process?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Author here. I changed some of the blackboxes for the 1951 event because some alert readers figured out a possible candidate for that event - which I liked so much I decided to make it canon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The flames shall consume their houses, yea, and their markets,

yea, dood, and their temples and stuff, maaan. puffs some 420-J

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: the revelation of the relationship between A and B was a perfect oh fuck moment, kudos. I only wonder what happens if you brainwash the mother into doing it against her will

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was the first SPC I ever read. I still find one of the most interesting, and definitely the most horrible, even more so than 110-Montauk.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This SCP is horrible. In all the best ways.
A bit cliché in some parts, but done in a way where that doesn't matter. And making the mother be forced to do it… and voluntarily at that… jeez.
Also,

Date of locution: April 4, 20██
Description of Type-S Event in Locution Event: [DATA EXPUNGED]
Type-S Event: [DATA EXPUNGED]
Outcome: Ongoing. Protocol M8 not yet executed.

The of a disaster happening at any time (or already happening, depending on who you ask) due to this thing makes this all the more interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Great SCP, and an easy +1 from me, but I have one small gripe- why are the locations of the events in the timeline ██████████'d? They're all real world events, that the foundation, any personnel, and the general population would be aware of. Even if they weren't initially aware, the information provided about the events and the dates make it easily possible to find information pertaining to them.

So it's an attempt at omitting data that doesn't actually omit anything, so as far as I'm concerned, the location names may as well be there.

Otherwise, great execution of what, in theory, is a clichéd concept. 'Sacrifice children at my alter, or I, the great <whatever> will rein terror and chaos on your people!' SHOULD be boring, but this really isn't. Great work.

Edit: a word

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Hey, if this prompted you to make crosslinks of your own, I'm happy. I can guarantee that your crosslinks will be more in line with your article than anything I can ever produce. Thank you for your consideration. Timer will be stopped.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
In that case, I am shooting down the specific proposed change - not because it isn't well-written (it is), but because (1) I kind of selfishly want every word of SCP-089 to be mine (*) and (2) most of the other Type-S events specifically mentioned in the article are specific real-world events, and the addition as worded would dilute that.

In the spirit of the cross-linking project, I'm going to think about adding a cross-link of my own. It might not be to 1746, though. (I'm a bit concerned about the tone whiplash from connecting grim-dark 089 to somewhat goofy black-comedy 1746).

(*) Don't interpret this as me withdrawing from the project. I'm still in. I'm just putting the brakes on this specific change based on the rationale described above.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: As author, you of course have the right to shoot down any and all things we are doing to your articles. For neutral responses, we'll be erring on the side of caution and making no changes.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: It is supposed to be that fire. If I got the date wrong, go ahead and fix that.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Hmm. No vote for now.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Fires eventually run out of things to burn. The famine was caused entirely by human actions, and could have been ended much slower and with more loss of life by conventional means. A cyclone only lasts a few hours or days. Its the same for the proposed change- there's always been another way, even if that meant just letting the thing run its course, with all the loss of life and suffering that entailed.

Also on a side note, I'm wondering if the major fire mentioned isn't supposed be dated to March 21st, 1788 rather than the 16th? There's one matching that description on the 21st, but not the 16th.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
I don't like this change. With other events being things that non-anomalous efforts were failing to deal with until the sacrifice is made make the sacrifices look more like a necessary evil. As the Foundation is now capable of stopping 1746, even if they weren't at the time, it makes it feel like the sacrifices are not the only way of stopping the Type-S events.

This change would not be a enough to effect my vote but I do feel it makes the article worse.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Edit: Timer removed, no edits made per author request (see discussion below).

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 72 hours if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

This SCP object is all about predicting cataclysmic level natural disasters that are only abated by playing ball with the objects infant sacrifice shtick. One SCP of note that is, in fact, a pretty massive natural disaster is spikebrennan's own SCP-1746. SCP-089 could have predicted an instance of SCP-1746 prior to 1966 (when the foundation found out the means of containing the storm), this instance of 1746 being dissipated in the end by 089. Recommending the following addition to SCP-089's Addendum #2:

Date of locution: October 15, 1948
Description of Type-S Event in Locution Event: “The heavens will open and its arrows shall rain down upon their cities and fields, reducing the earth to glass.”
Type-S Event: Violent storm pattern consisting of hundreds of simultaneous lightning strikes concurrent with episodes of ball lightning, and hurricane-force wind gusts over the Caribbean Island of ███████. Likely an instance of SCP-1746.
Outcome: Protocol M8 completed on day 2 after locution event. Resultant weather pattern devastated most of the vessels with in the island's harbors and resulted in approximately ████ casualties.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: [[http://3.bp.blogspot.com/-MzMsZL6GwLU/U5jycM4EUZI/AAAAAAAANk8/2ZtKcMbGlSg/s1600/moloch01.jpg]]

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a really good SCP to me but there is one thing, that bugs me. In the description it says, that a forced or false execution of Protocol M8

invalidate the attempted completion of the protocol and are known to intensify the severity of the attendant Type-S Event.

But if SCP-089 does only anticipate and not cause them as Dr. Patel speculated, how will it intensify the Type-S Event?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "420" was a cannabis culture "meme" starting in 1971 (in San Rafael CA, by a group called the "Waldos"). It predates the internet (not ARPANET but MLG-like shenanigans were illegal there) by over a decade.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Both "420" and "M8" are MLG memes, look it up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Considering the last entry, shouldn't this be Euclid Keter, with Protocol M9 well under way?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Actually, that is still somewhat controversial. Substantial unburned remains have been found at altars to Moloch, and did not appear to have any markers of disease, defect, or other common natural caused of infant mortality. Dating shows them to be most commonly associated with known periods of severe hardship (droughts, etc.). Chances are strong that these were actual human child sacrifices, made during times of desperation, with reports exaggerated by the Romans, rather than invented; but again, no conclusive physical evidence. It's also possible that they died from the effects of said hardships (eg. starvation) and were subsequently cremated as an offering to propitiate the god and end the hardship.

Definitely getting a +1 from me. Excellent execution of a difficult subject.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Scantron, I'm being devoured by curiosity here. What do you mean???? ;>

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've read somewhere that the whole baby-burning was Roman propaganda during the Punic wars (kind of like "blood libel" and "Baphomet"). There's evidence that they did cremate babies in Carthage, but probably when they had already died (of normal infant mortality reasons).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
4, 20

M8

moloch confirmed mlg

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Date of locution: April 4, 20██
Description of Type-S Event in Locution Event: [DATA EXPUNGED]
Type-S Event: [DATA EXPUNGED]
Outcome: Ongoing. Protocol M8 not yet executed.

OH FUCK
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What the hell is wrong with you, you sick fuck? +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I hate to love this SCP…
But I do anyway.

Excellent basic idea, flawless execution.
Oh… I did believe that the ancient god who demanded child sacrifice was Baal, but no. I checked. I bow before your superior wisdom, Spikebrennan!

Upvote, damn you! ;>

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Imagine training all your life to convince a mother to kill her child. Then imagine having to live with that. Another +1.

(A little miffed by "Euclid (see below)"; is there any reason for that to be there?)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This has always been one of my favorite SCPs. The idea is haunting enough but the way it's executed just makes it all the better/worse. A few things I thought about it reading it again:

- Does the mother have to be present for the entire time the child is burning to death? The article mentions restraint and horns to mask the screams, but can they take her away (provided she doesn't resist) as soon as Protocol M8 is done? If not…two to five hours of watching your child die. Hip hip hooray.

- I also thought "so is the statue open or closed for the burning" but without oxygen the fire couldn't burn. Open it is.

- Finally, what really gets me about this one is the long periods of time between locution events. Mu-89 seems to only exist for this one SCP, and while they're pretty busy right now, imagine just going about your life, checking on 89 for thirty seconds a day and finding nothing again and again, spending the rest of your time living, researching, but also waiting, wondering, not even knowing if you'll be needed at all in your lifetime for this, but always being ready, always having to prepare yourself… and then one morning it talks and you have to drop everything else and go.

So basically +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
When I first read this a year and a half ago, the visceral horror was an intellectual thing.

Reading it now that I'm a father, it reaches down through my brain into my spine and twists.

Take my +1, you monster.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That probably explains the ongoing event.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maybe this is historical. The event of 1850 is Taiping Heavenly Kingdom Insurgency.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow. This is easily one of the best articles I've ever read. I read this a while ago and though "meh", but I think it was late at night and I didn't quite comprehend what I was reading. One line in particular gave me serious chills:

It is also recommended (although not a required part of Protocol M8) to cause the execution of step 2 of Protocol M8 to be accompanied by the sounding of horns and percussion instruments, as doing so may mask the sounds made by SCP-089-A during the execution of the protocol.

So does the Foundation just keep a wind ensemble around to mask the sounds of sacrificing children? Do they have musicians on hand to deal with things like that? Or do personnel just do it themselves, regardless of ability? That last possibility really creeps me out. Imagine a small group of doctors and other personnel banging on timpani and trying to make a decent sound with brass instruments in an attempt to not hear what is going on. +1

item.title: Re: IRL photo
item.content:encoded:
Well but if the photo is uploaded to wikidot isn't it under public domain?

Edit: BTW I didn't want to get it removed, I only wanted to give some information about his backstory, for anyone interested where the photo came from.

item.title: Re: IRL photo
item.content:encoded:
Thank you for pointing this out. I checked, and the film appears to be under Public Domain; the image does not need to be removed.

Edit: Scratch that. Actually, if it's a picture that someone took, then it's not under Public Domain. The Image Team will look into this.

item.title: IRL photo
item.content:encoded:
I don't know if this could be useful or not, but the photo is taken from the Museum of Cinema, in Turin, Italy. It comes from an old silent movie called "Cabiria" whose screenplay was written by the fascist period writer Gabriele d'Annunzio. The name "cabiria" according to d'annunzio means "born from the fire", and it's set during the punic wars. Here's the Wikipedia link to it. Hope it could be useful by some mean.

http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cabiria

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've wondered about this as well. To be honest, I sort of figured that if all attempts at persuading them failed and the mother just completely refused, the SCP would eventually declare a new -A and -B. It says the child is always between eight months and six years old, so what happens if they outgrow that window? Are they still "eligible" for sacrifice, or is the procedure screwed up forever? Even if it still works, I'm not sure how big 0.6 cubic meters is. Could it fit an adult, if it took that long to convince the mother? And as someone mentioned earlier, what if one or both of them died from other causes before the procedure could be carried out?

If the SCP is benevolent (i.e., it predicts and ends the disasters, but doesn't cause them), then it would probably ensure there would always be a way to stop them. If it's malevolent, I imagine it would still do that as a method of self-preservation, because the risk of destroying it and then finding out that the procedure is still necessary would be moot if it became impossible by any means to carry it out. However, that's assuming a certain level of sentience on the part of the statue, or that the entity behind it either is the statue or would be harmed if it were destroyed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It seems odd that there is no record of Procedure M8 yet failing outright, given that coercion is impossible. You’d think that out of a random selection of mothers, many would either lack the necessary credulity (“Magic isn’t real! Especially child-murdering doom magic! What the fuck is wrong with you people!”) or empathy (“So what if your silly statue kills all the [REDACTEDS] in [REDACTED]? They’re all terrorists anyway!”) to voluntarily execute Procedure M8.
There are three fridge horror options here:
- MTF Mu-89 is preternaturally good at its job. And its job is to convince people to murder their loved ones. Makes you wonder how narrow the definition of “coercion” is.
- SCP-089 intentionally selects victims who will believe its claims, and who are too empathetic to allow the attendant event to play out, but who also love their children. Which adds an extra soupcon of cruelty to the skip.
- SCP-089, and the Foundation, have been lucky so far, and that luck will run out.

tldr; All it takes is one casually racist mother to ruin everything.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Procedure M8?

"Ey M8, ya think ye can puts ya kiddie in mah furnace? It gives meh ah twizzy."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There is no SCP that managed to lodge itself quite so deeply in my mind than this one. Thank you, good sir!
whooaaaa nice one, makes my brain wonder what that last event is….

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Holy shit, #2 is the Taiping Rebellion!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I had about sixteen moments where my brain thought "you just earned an upvote" while reading this thing. Awesomely done, absolutely terrifying.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While I'm surprised at the high praise it's received, I do think it's okay. My only qualm is that the whole "mother willingly giving child as sacrifice" seems a bit over-the-top. Especially the specification that they must have a close bond and such. What really gets me in this one is the last Type-S event ongoing since the year 20XX, because it immediately makes you wonder just what disaster(s) you've seen could be the result. It's that little tidbit at the end that earns it my +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Rereading it, yeah, no, I don't like it. Mainly the stupid, out-of-place Howl quote, but also it just… I don't know, it feels like simultaneous poor form and overambition. Maintaining my -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: With all due respect to 87, 93, and 96, this is my favorite SCP out of the first hundred.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just been brought here 'cos of the recent tale including this skip and can't believe I hadn't read it before. Although, 1) As mentioned, shouldn't this be keter and 2) If the events go on indefinitely until the procedure is done then how do they know that it doesn't work if the subject is forced to do it? If you put a gun to someones head and persuade them to kill their kid then does the type S disaster carry on forever? I kinda like that idea because it makes me think that this thing invented AIDS.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This article rubs me the wrong way. I know that probably sounds hypocritical since I wrote the only other SCP that requires human immolation, but I'm just way too disturbed by thought of a mother burning her child alive. You win the upvote from me for making me hate this thing so much.

+1 you bastard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is very well executed. When I first started reading, I couldn't help but roll my eyes. "Oh boy," I thought, "another Thing Where We Have To Do A Bad Thing Or Else A Worse Thing Happens." However, the idea that it's only reporting on and stopping the event if appeased, rather than being responsible for the event, as well as the touch that the event doesn't affect the area where -A and -B live, makes this very good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So it's implicated in the New Orleans fire of 1788, the Chinese peasant uprising in 1850, and the Bangladesh cyclone in 1970. Can't seem to find a four-letter place with an earthquake and volcano in 1951, was that because it was prevented? Also nice quote from Allen Ginsberg's poem Howl, it makes the SCP seem far more confusing, as in, when was it really created? Was Allen inspired by it or did he somehow create it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted because every damn time I read this I get chills. I mean this is just brilliantly sick horror.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Possibility 3: The thing causing the disaster's is using the statue as a speaker, and an offering point.

Don't destroy it: No change.

Destroy it: Moloch gets pissed off, and causes something massive.
